headline: Eli Lilly Extends AC Immune Tau Pact To Broaden Alzheimer’s Pipeline Potential
summary: Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) and AC Immune have amended their collaboration focused on Tau aggregation inhibitor small molecules for Alzheimer's disease and related neurodegenerative diseases. The updated agreement centers on a next generation Tau aggregation inhibitor program, with plans for IND enabling studies in 2026. The move extends Eli Lilly's R&D activity beyond obesity and diabetes into neurodegeneration, an area with significant unmet medical need. For investors who mostly associate Eli...

headline: How Novo Nordisk's weight loss pill is drawing in new patients for obesity treatment
summary: Novo Nordisk's latest GLP-1 weight loss drug comes in pill form. Its ease of access has drawn in new obesity patients who may have opted against its injectable predecessor. After the drug's first few months on the market, it faces fresh competition following FDA approval of Eli Lilly's rival oral weight loss pill. CNBC's Annika Kim Constantino breaks down how this medicine is impacting the pharmaceutical industry.

headline: How The Eli Lilly (LLY) Obesity And Diabetes Story Is Reframing The Investment Narrative
summary: Eli Lilly’s modelled fair value has been trimmed slightly from US$1,211.21 to US$1,209.21 per share, a very small move that still attracted attention given the stock’s high profile in obesity and diabetes. That modest change sits alongside research that focuses on GLP-1 pricing, new approvals such as Foundayo, and differing views on how much of the obesity opportunity is already reflected in current valuations. As you read on, you will see how to track these shifting assumptions and keep up...
